I'm working on the Project Component Master system for Clevertech (an ERPNext app for engineer-to-order manufacturing).

Please read these critical documents first:
1. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/clevertech_context.md
2. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/docs/project_component_master_tech_specs.md

Also read the existing implementation files:
3. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/project_component_master/bom_hooks.py
4. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/project_component_master/bulk_generation.py
5. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/public/js/project.js
6. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/hooks.py

And the existing BOM Upload code we need to import from (DO NOT modify this file):
7. /home/bharatbodh/bharatbodh-bench/apps/clevertech/clevertech/clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload.py

Current Status:
- ✅ Phase 1: DocTypes created (Project Component Master, Component BOM Usage, Component Procurement Record)
- ✅ Phase 1A: BOM hooks implemented (on_submit, on_cancel, on_update_after_submit)
- ✅ Phase 2A: Bulk generation from existing BOMs (cutover flow, tested on SMR260001)
- ⏳ Phase 2: Enhanced BOM Upload — "Create BOMs with Validation" button (see Decision 11 & 12 in context doc)
- ⏳ Phase 3: Procurement hooks — Material Request, RFQ, Purchase Order, Purchase Receipt

Key Design Decisions to follow:
- Decision 11: Two flows — cutover (BOM→CM) vs new projects (CM→BOM)
- Decision 12: New button on BOM Upload, import existing functions, do NOT modify bom_upload.py
- Decision 3A: BOM versioning uses ERPNext native (no custom versioning)

Next Tasks:
1. Implement bom_upload_enhanced.py (new "Create BOMs with Validation" button)
   - Import parse_rows, build_tree, ensure_item_exists, create_bom_recursive from bom_upload.py
   - Sequence: Items → Component Masters → Analysis → BOMs → Link back
   - Handle blocking logic and change detection
2. Implement procurement hooks for tracking Material Request, Purchase Order, Purchase Receipt
   in Component Procurement Record child table
This prompt gives the next session full context on what's done, what's pending, which files to read, and which design decisions to follow.

""" Bulk Generation of Component Masters from Existing BOMs Provides functionality to create Project Component Master records for all items with BOMs in a project. This is useful for migrating existing projects into the Component Master system. """ import frappe from frappe import _ from clevertech.project_component_master.bom_hooks import ( calculate_bom_structure_hash, add_or_update_bom_usage, ) @frappe.whitelist() def generate_component_masters_from_boms(project): """ Generate Component Master records for all items with BOMs in a project. Args: project: Project name Returns: dict: Summary with created_count, skipped_count, and details list """ if not frappe.has_permission("Project Component Master", "create"): frappe.throw(_("No permission to create Component Masters")) # Validate project exists if not frappe.db.exists("Project", project): frappe.throw(_("Project {0} does not exist").format(project)) # Get all submitted BOMs for this project boms = frappe.get_all( "BOM", filters={ "project": project, "docstatus": 1, # Only submitted BOMs "is_active": 1, # Only active BOMs "is_default": 1 # Only default BOMs for each item }, fields=["name", "item", "item_name"], order_by="item" ) if not boms: frappe.msgprint( _("No active BOMs found for project {0}").format(project), title=_("No BOMs Found"), indicator="orange" ) return { "created_count": 0, "skipped_count": 0, "details": [] } created_count = 0 skipped_count = 0 details = [] for bom in boms: item_code = bom.item bom_name = bom.name # Check if Component Master already exists for this project + item existing = frappe.db.exists( "Project Component Master", {"project": project, "item_code": item_code} ) if existing: skipped_count += 1 details.append({ "item_code": item_code, "bom": bom_name, "status": "Skipped - Already exists", "component_master": existing }) continue # Create new Component Master try: component_master = frappe.get_doc({ "doctype": "Project Component Master", "project": project, "item_code": item_code, "has_bom": 1, "active_bom": bom_name, "is_loose_item": 0, # BOMs are managed components, not loose items "loose_qty_required": 0, "design_status": "Design Released" }) # Calculate and set BOM structure hash bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name) component_master.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc) # Save with flags to skip validation during bulk creation component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.insert(ignore_permissions=True) created_count += 1 details.append({ "item_code": item_code, "bom": bom_name, "status": "Created", "component_master": component_master.name }) except Exception as e: frappe.log_error( title=f"Failed to create Component Master for {item_code}", message=frappe.get_traceback() ) skipped_count += 1 details.append({ "item_code": item_code, "bom": bom_name, "status": f"Error: {str(e)}", "component_master": None }) # Now populate BOM Usage tables by calling BOM hooks for each created Component Master if created_count > 0: populate_bom_usage_tables(project, boms) # Commit the transaction frappe.db.commit() # Show summary message message = _("Component Masters Generated:

") message += _("✓ Created: {0}
").format(created_count) message += _("○ Skipped: {0}
").format(skipped_count) message += _("━ Total BOMs: {0}").format(len(boms)) frappe.msgprint( message, title=_("Bulk Generation Complete"), indicator="green" ) return { "created_count": created_count, "skipped_count": skipped_count, "details": details } def populate_bom_usage_tables(project, boms): """ Populate BOM Usage child tables for all Component Masters in the project. This simulates what the BOM hooks would have done if they existed when BOMs were created. Args: project: Project name boms: List of BOM dicts with name and item fields """ for bom in boms: bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom.name) # For each child item in the BOM, add BOM usage entry for item in bom_doc.items: add_or_update_bom_usage( project=project, item_code=item.item_code, parent_bom=bom.name, qty_per_unit=item.qty )

content.content: 1→""" 2→BOM Event Hooks for Project Component Master 3→ 4→Handles updating BOM Usage child table when BOMs are created, updated, or cancelled. 5→Uses "silent skip" pattern - only updates if Component Master exists for the component. 6→Shows info message when project BOMs are not tracked (helps catch accidental omissions). 7→""" 8→ 9→import frappe 10→from frappe import _ 11→import hashlib 12→import json 13→ 14→ 15→def on_bom_submit(doc, method=None): 16→ """ 17→ Called when a BOM is submitted. 18→ Updates BOM Usage table for all items in the BOM that have Component Masters. 19→ Shows info message if this is a project BOM that's not tracked. 20→ """ 21→ if not doc.project: 22→ # No project linked - skip silently (standard product BOM) 23→ return 24→ 25→ # Check if this BOM itself is tracked in Component Master 26→ component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) 27→ if not component_master: 28→ # Project BOM but not tracked - inform user 29→ frappe.msgprint( 30→ _("This BOM is linked to project {0} but is not tracked in Project Component Master. Procurement monitoring is not enabled for {1}.").format( 31→ doc.project, doc.item 32→ ), 33→ title=_("BOM Not Tracked for Procurement"), 34→ indicator="blue", 35→ alert=True 36→ ) 37→ return # Silent skip for processing 38→ 39→ # Process all child items in the BOM 40→ for item in doc.items: 41→ add_or_update_bom_usage( 42→ project=doc.project, 43→ item_code=item.item_code, 44→ parent_bom=doc.name, 45→ qty_per_unit=item.qty 46→ ) 47→ 48→ # Update BOM structure hash and related fields for the BOM item itself 49→ update_component_master_bom_fields( 50→ project=doc.project, 51→ item_code=doc.item, 52→ bom_name=doc.name 53→ ) 54→ 55→ 56→def on_bom_cancel(doc, method=None): 57→ """ 58→ Called when a BOM is cancelled. 59→ Removes BOM Usage entries for all items in this BOM. 60→ Silent skip if not a project BOM or not tracked. 61→ """ 62→ if not doc.project: 63→ return 64→ 65→ # Check if this BOM is tracked 66→ component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) 67→ if not component_master: 68→ return # Silent skip 69→ 70→ # Remove BOM usage entries for all child items in the BOM 71→ for item in doc.items: 72→ remove_bom_usage( 73→ project=doc.project, 74→ item_code=item.item_code, 75→ parent_bom=doc.name 76→ ) 77→ 78→ # Clear BOM fields from the Component Master for the BOM item itself 79→ clear_component_master_bom_fields( 80→ project=doc.project, 81→ item_code=doc.item 82→ ) 83→ 84→ 85→def on_bom_update(doc, method=None): 86→ """ 87→ Called when a BOM is updated after submission. 88→ Refreshes BOM Usage entries and checks for structure changes. 89→ Silent skip if not a project BOM or not tracked. 90→ """ 91→ if not doc.project: 92→ return 93→ 94→ # Get the Component Master for this BOM's item 95→ component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) 96→ if not component_master: 97→ return # Silent skip - no Component Master exists 98→ 99→ # Calculate new BOM structure hash 100→ new_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(doc) 101→ old_hash = component_master.bom_structure_hash 102→ 103→ # If structure changed, flag for review 104→ if old_hash and new_hash != old_hash: 105→ frappe.msgprint( 106→ _("BOM structure has changed for {0}. Component Master {1} may need review.").format( 107→ doc.item, component_master.name 108→ ), 109→ title=_("BOM Structure Changed"), 110→ indicator="orange", 111→ alert=True 112→ ) 113→ 114→ # Refresh BOM usage entries (remove old, add new) 115→ # This handles items added/removed from BOM 116→ refresh_bom_usage(doc) 117→ 118→ # Update hash and other BOM fields 119→ update_component_master_bom_fields( 120→ project=doc.project, 121→ item_code=doc.item, 122→ bom_name=doc.name 123→ ) 124→ 125→ 126→# Helper Functions 127→ 128→def get_component_master(project, item_code): 129→ """ 130→ Get Component Master for a project and item. 131→ Returns None if doesn't exist (silent skip pattern). 132→ """ 133→ try: 134→ name = frappe.db.get_value( 135→ "Project Component Master", 136→ {"project": project, "item_code": item_code}, 137→ "name" 138→ ) 139→ if name: 140→ return frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", name) 141→ return None 142→ except Exception: 143→ return None 144→ 145→ 146→def add_or_update_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom, qty_per_unit): 147→ """ 148→ Add or update a BOM Usage entry in the Component Master. 149→ Silent skip if Component Master doesn't exist. 150→ """ 151→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 152→ if not component_master: 153→ return # Silent skip 154→ 155→ # Check if this BOM usage already exists 156→ existing_row = None 157→ for row in component_master.bom_usage: 158→ if row.parent_bom == parent_bom: 159→ existing_row = row 160→ break 161→ 162→ if existing_row: 163→ # Update existing row 164→ if existing_row.qty_per_unit != qty_per_unit: 165→ existing_row.qty_per_unit = qty_per_unit 166→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 167→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 168→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 169→ 170→ frappe.msgprint( 171→ _("Updated BOM Usage in Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), 172→ indicator="green", 173→ alert=True 174→ ) 175→ else: 176→ # Add new row 177→ component_master.append("bom_usage", { 178→ "parent_bom": parent_bom, 179→ "qty_per_unit": qty_per_unit 180→ }) 181→ 182→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 183→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 184→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 185→ 186→ frappe.msgprint( 187→ _("Added BOM Usage to Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), 188→ indicator="green", 189→ alert=True 190→ ) 191→ 192→ 193→def remove_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom): 194→ """ 195→ Remove a BOM Usage entry from the Component Master. 196→ Silent skip if Component Master doesn't exist. 197→ """ 198→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 199→ if not component_master: 200→ return # Silent skip 201→ 202→ # Find and remove the row 203→ rows_to_remove = [] 204→ for idx, row in enumerate(component_master.bom_usage): 205→ if row.parent_bom == parent_bom: 206→ rows_to_remove.append(idx) 207→ 208→ # Remove in reverse order to maintain indices 209→ for idx in reversed(rows_to_remove): 210→ component_master.remove(component_master.bom_usage[idx]) 211→ 212→ if rows_to_remove: 213→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 214→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 215→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 216→ 217→ frappe.msgprint( 218→ _("Removed BOM Usage from Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), 219→ indicator="blue", 220→ alert=True 221→ ) 222→ 223→ 224→def refresh_bom_usage(bom_doc): 225→ """ 226→ Refresh all BOM usage entries for a BOM. 227→ Removes entries for items no longer in BOM, adds/updates current items. 228→ """ 229→ if not bom_doc.project: 230→ return 231→ 232→ # Get current item codes in the BOM 233→ current_items = {item.item_code: item.qty for item in bom_doc.items} 234→ 235→ # Find all Component Masters that reference this BOM 236→ component_masters = frappe.get_all( 237→ "Project Component Master", 238→ filters={"project": bom_doc.project}, 239→ fields=["name", "item_code"] 240→ ) 241→ 242→ for cm in component_masters: 243→ component_master = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm.name) 244→ 245→ # Check if this Component Master has a BOM usage entry for this BOM 246→ has_changes = False 247→ rows_to_remove = [] 248→ 249→ for idx, row in enumerate(component_master.bom_usage): 250→ if row.parent_bom == bom_doc.name: 251→ # This Component Master references the updated BOM 252→ if cm.item_code in current_items: 253→ # Item still in BOM - update qty if changed 254→ if row.qty_per_unit != current_items[cm.item_code]: 255→ row.qty_per_unit = current_items[cm.item_code] 256→ has_changes = True 257→ else: 258→ # Item no longer in BOM - mark for removal 259→ rows_to_remove.append(idx) 260→ has_changes = True 261→ 262→ # Remove obsolete rows 263→ for idx in reversed(rows_to_remove): 264→ component_master.remove(component_master.bom_usage[idx]) 265→ 266→ # Save if changes made 267→ if has_changes: 268→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 269→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 270→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 271→ 272→ 273→def update_component_master_bom_fields(project, item_code, bom_name): 274→ """ 275→ Update BOM-related fields in Component Master: 276→ - has_bom 277→ - active_bom 278→ - bom_structure_hash 279→ """ 280→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 281→ if not component_master: 282→ return # Silent skip 283→ 284→ # Get the BOM document 285→ bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name) 286→ 287→ # Update fields 288→ component_master.has_bom = 1 289→ component_master.active_bom = bom_name 290→ component_master.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc) 291→ 292→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 293→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 294→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 295→ 296→ 297→def clear_component_master_bom_fields(project, item_code): 298→ """ 299→ Clear BOM fields when BOM is cancelled. 300→ """ 301→ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) 302→ if not component_master: 303→ return # Silent skip 304→ 305→ component_master.has_bom = 0 306→ component_master.active_bom = None 307→ component_master.bom_structure_hash = None 308→ 309→ component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True 310→ component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True 311→ component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) 312→ 313→ 314→def calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc): 315→ """ 316→ Calculate MD5 hash of BOM structure. 317→ Based on sorted list of (item_code, qty) tuples. 318→ """ 319→ if not bom_doc.items: 320→ return None 321→ 322→ # Create list of (item_code, qty) tuples, sorted by item_code 323→ structure = sorted( 324→ [(item.item_code, float(item.qty)) for item in bom_doc.items], 325→ key=lambda x: x[0] 326→ ) 327→ 328→ # Convert to JSON string and hash 329→ structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True) 330→ return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest() 331→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. file.content: """ BOM Event Hooks for Project Component Master Handles updating BOM Usage child table when BOMs are created, updated, or cancelled. Uses "silent skip" pattern - only updates if Component Master exists for the component. Shows info message when project BOMs are not tracked (helps catch accidental omissions). """ import frappe from frappe import _ import hashlib import json def on_bom_submit(doc, method=None): """ Called when a BOM is submitted. Updates BOM Usage table for all items in the BOM that have Component Masters. Shows info message if this is a project BOM that's not tracked. """ if not doc.project: # No project linked - skip silently (standard product BOM) return # Check if this BOM itself is tracked in Component Master component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) if not component_master: # Project BOM but not tracked - inform user frappe.msgprint( _("This BOM is linked to project {0} but is not tracked in Project Component Master. Procurement monitoring is not enabled for {1}.").format( doc.project, doc.item ), title=_("BOM Not Tracked for Procurement"), indicator="blue", alert=True ) return # Silent skip for processing # Process all child items in the BOM for item in doc.items: add_or_update_bom_usage( project=doc.project, item_code=item.item_code, parent_bom=doc.name, qty_per_unit=item.qty ) # Update BOM structure hash and related fields for the BOM item itself update_component_master_bom_fields( project=doc.project, item_code=doc.item, bom_name=doc.name ) def on_bom_cancel(doc, method=None): """ Called when a BOM is cancelled. Removes BOM Usage entries for all items in this BOM. Silent skip if not a project BOM or not tracked. """ if not doc.project: return # Check if this BOM is tracked component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) if not component_master: return # Silent skip # Remove BOM usage entries for all child items in the BOM for item in doc.items: remove_bom_usage( project=doc.project, item_code=item.item_code, parent_bom=doc.name ) # Clear BOM fields from the Component Master for the BOM item itself clear_component_master_bom_fields( project=doc.project, item_code=doc.item ) def on_bom_update(doc, method=None): """ Called when a BOM is updated after submission. Refreshes BOM Usage entries and checks for structure changes. Silent skip if not a project BOM or not tracked. """ if not doc.project: return # Get the Component Master for this BOM's item component_master = get_component_master(doc.project, doc.item) if not component_master: return # Silent skip - no Component Master exists # Calculate new BOM structure hash new_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(doc) old_hash = component_master.bom_structure_hash # If structure changed, flag for review if old_hash and new_hash != old_hash: frappe.msgprint( _("BOM structure has changed for {0}. Component Master {1} may need review.").format( doc.item, component_master.name ), title=_("BOM Structure Changed"), indicator="orange", alert=True ) # Refresh BOM usage entries (remove old, add new) # This handles items added/removed from BOM refresh_bom_usage(doc) # Update hash and other BOM fields update_component_master_bom_fields( project=doc.project, item_code=doc.item, bom_name=doc.name ) # Helper Functions def get_component_master(project, item_code): """ Get Component Master for a project and item. Returns None if doesn't exist (silent skip pattern). """ try: name = frappe.db.get_value( "Project Component Master", {"project": project, "item_code": item_code}, "name" ) if name: return frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", name) return None except Exception: return None def add_or_update_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom, qty_per_unit): """ Add or update a BOM Usage entry in the Component Master. Silent skip if Component Master doesn't exist. """ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) if not component_master: return # Silent skip # Check if this BOM usage already exists existing_row = None for row in component_master.bom_usage: if row.parent_bom == parent_bom: existing_row = row break if existing_row: # Update existing row if existing_row.qty_per_unit != qty_per_unit: existing_row.qty_per_unit = qty_per_unit component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) frappe.msgprint( _("Updated BOM Usage in Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), indicator="green", alert=True ) else: # Add new row component_master.append("bom_usage", { "parent_bom": parent_bom, "qty_per_unit": qty_per_unit }) component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) frappe.msgprint( _("Added BOM Usage to Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), indicator="green", alert=True ) def remove_bom_usage(project, item_code, parent_bom): """ Remove a BOM Usage entry from the Component Master. Silent skip if Component Master doesn't exist. """ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) if not component_master: return # Silent skip # Find and remove the row rows_to_remove = [] for idx, row in enumerate(component_master.bom_usage): if row.parent_bom == parent_bom: rows_to_remove.append(idx) # Remove in reverse order to maintain indices for idx in reversed(rows_to_remove): component_master.remove(component_master.bom_usage[idx]) if rows_to_remove: component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) frappe.msgprint( _("Removed BOM Usage from Component Master {0}").format(component_master.name), indicator="blue", alert=True ) def refresh_bom_usage(bom_doc): """ Refresh all BOM usage entries for a BOM. Removes entries for items no longer in BOM, adds/updates current items. """ if not bom_doc.project: return # Get current item codes in the BOM current_items = {item.item_code: item.qty for item in bom_doc.items} # Find all Component Masters that reference this BOM component_masters = frappe.get_all( "Project Component Master", filters={"project": bom_doc.project}, fields=["name", "item_code"] ) for cm in component_masters: component_master = frappe.get_doc("Project Component Master", cm.name) # Check if this Component Master has a BOM usage entry for this BOM has_changes = False rows_to_remove = [] for idx, row in enumerate(component_master.bom_usage): if row.parent_bom == bom_doc.name: # This Component Master references the updated BOM if cm.item_code in current_items: # Item still in BOM - update qty if changed if row.qty_per_unit != current_items[cm.item_code]: row.qty_per_unit = current_items[cm.item_code] has_changes = True else: # Item no longer in BOM - mark for removal rows_to_remove.append(idx) has_changes = True # Remove obsolete rows for idx in reversed(rows_to_remove): component_master.remove(component_master.bom_usage[idx]) # Save if changes made if has_changes: component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) def update_component_master_bom_fields(project, item_code, bom_name): """ Update BOM-related fields in Component Master: - has_bom - active_bom - bom_structure_hash """ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) if not component_master: return # Silent skip # Get the BOM document bom_doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM", bom_name) # Update fields component_master.has_bom = 1 component_master.active_bom = bom_name component_master.bom_structure_hash = calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc) component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) def clear_component_master_bom_fields(project, item_code): """ Clear BOM fields when BOM is cancelled. """ component_master = get_component_master(project, item_code) if not component_master: return # Silent skip component_master.has_bom = 0 component_master.active_bom = None component_master.bom_structure_hash = None component_master.flags.ignore_validate = True component_master.flags.ignore_mandatory = True component_master.save(ignore_permissions=True) def calculate_bom_structure_hash(bom_doc): """ Calculate MD5 hash of BOM structure. Based on sorted list of (item_code, qty) tuples. """ if not bom_doc.items: return None # Create list of (item_code, qty) tuples, sorted by item_code structure = sorted( [(item.item_code, float(item.qty)) for item in bom_doc.items], key=lambda x: x[0] ) # Convert to JSON string and hash structure_str = json.dumps(structure, sort_keys=True) return hashlib.md5(structure_str.encode()).hexdigest()

1→import frappe
     2→from frappe.model.document import Document
     3→import openpyxl
     4→import io
     5→
     6→
     7→class BOMUpload(Document):
     8→    pass
     9→
    10→
    11→@frappe.whitelist()
    12→def create_boms(docname):
    13→    doc = frappe.get_doc("BOM Upload", docname)
    14→    return _create_boms(doc)
    15→
    16→
    17→def _create_boms(doc):
    18→
    19→    file_doc = frappe.get_doc("File", {"file_url": doc.bom_file})
    20→    content = file_doc.get_content()
    21→
    22→    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content), data_only=True)
    23→    ws = wb.active
    24→
    25→    rows = parse_rows(ws)
    26→    tree = build_tree(rows)
    27→
    28→    created = 0
    29→    skipped = 0
    30→    failed = 0
    31→    errors = []
    32→
    33→    for node in tree:
    34→        try:
    35→            created_flag = create_bom_recursive(node, doc.project)
    36→            if created_flag:
    37→                created += 1
    38→            else:
    39→                skipped += 1
    40→        except Exception as e:
    41→            failed += 1
    42→            errors.append(f"{node['item_code']} → {str(e)}")
    43→
    44→    return {
    45→        "created": created,
    46→        "skipped": skipped,
    47→        "failed": failed,
    48→        "errors": errors
    49→    }
    50→
    51→
    52→# ================= Excel helpers =================
    53→
    54→def clean_code(code):
    55→    if not code:
    56→        return None
    57→    return str(code).replace(".", "").strip()
    58→
    59→
    60→def to_float(val, default=0):
    61→    try:
    62→        return float(val)
    63→    except Exception:
    64→        return default
    65→
    66→
    67→def normalize_uom(uom):
    68→    if not uom:
    69→        return "Nos"
    70→    u = str(uom).strip().upper()
    71→    return {"NUMERI": "Nos", "PEZZI": "Nos", "METRI": "Meter"}.get(u, u)
    72→
    73→
    74→# ================= Parse Excel =================
    75→
    76→def parse_rows(ws):
    77→
    78→    rows = []
    79→
    80→    for r in range(3, ws.max_row + 1):
    81→
    82→        code_raw = ws[f"C{r}"].value
    83→        if not code_raw:
    84→            continue
    85→
    86→        position = ws[f"A{r}"].value or ws[f"B{r}"].value
    87→
    88→        rows.append({
    89→            "position": position,
    90→            "item_code": clean_code(code_raw),
    91→            "description": ws[f"D{r}"].value,
    92→            "qty": to_float(ws[f"E{r}"].value, 1),
    93→            "revision": ws[f"G{r}"].value,
    94→            "material": ws[f"AC{r}"].value,
    95→            "part_number": ws[f"AD{r}"].value,
    96→            "weight": to_float(ws[f"AE{r}"].value, 0),
    97→            "manufacturer": ws[f"AF{r}"].value,
    98→            "treatment": ws[f"AL{r}"].value,
    99→            "uom": normalize_uom(ws[f"AM{r}"].value),
   100→            "level": int(ws[f"AR{r}"].value or 0),
   101→            "children": []
   102→        })
   103→
   104→    return rows
   105→
   106→
   107→# ================= Hierarchy =================
   108→
   109→def build_tree(rows):
   110→
   111→    stack = []
   112→    roots = []
   113→
   114→    for row in rows:
   115→
   116→        while stack and stack[-1]["level"] >= row["level"]:
   117→            stack.pop()
   118→
   119→        if stack:
   120→            stack[-1]["children"].append(row)
   121→        else:
   122→            roots.append(row)
   123→
   124→        stack.append(row)
   125→
   126→    return roots
   127→
   128→
   129→# ================= Items =================
   130→
   131→def ensure_item_exists(item_code, description, uom):
   132→
   133→    if frappe.db.exists("Item", item_code):
   134→        return
   135→
   136→    item_name = description or item_code
   137→
   138→    item = frappe.get_doc({
   139→        "doctype": "Item",
   140→        "item_code": item_code,
   141→        "item_name": item_name,
   142→        "description": item_name,
   143→        "item_group": "All Item Groups",
   144→        "stock_uom": uom or "Nos",
   145→        "is_stock_item": 1,
   146→        "is_purchase_item": 1,
   147→        "is_sales_item": 0
   148→    })
   149→
   150→    item.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
   151→
   152→
   153→# ================= BOM =================
   154→def create_bom_recursive(node, project, root_level=None):
   155→    """Create BOMs recursively, creating child BOMs first (bottom-up)"""
   156→    
   157→    if root_level is None:
   158→        root_level = node["level"]
   159→
   160→    ensure_item_exists(node["item_code"], node["description"], node["uom"])
   161→
   162→    # First, recursively create BOMs for all children that have sub-assemblies
   163→    for child in node["children"]:
   164→        if child["children"]:  # If child has its own children, it needs a BOM
   165→            create_bom_recursive(child, project, child["level"])
   166→
   167→    # Check if BOM already exists
   168→    existing = frappe.db.exists("BOM", {
   169→        "item": node["item_code"],
   170→        "is_active": 1,
   171→        "is_default": 1
   172→    })
   173→
   174→    if existing:
   175→        return False
   176→
   177→    # Create BOM for current node
   178→    bom = frappe.get_doc({
   179→        "doctype": "BOM",
   180→        "item": node["item_code"],
   181→        "quantity": 1,
   182→        "project": project,
   183→        "is_active": 1,
   184→        "is_default": 1
   185→    })
   186→
   187→    # Add ONLY direct children (not grandchildren)
   188→    for child in node["children"]:
   189→        ensure_item_exists(child["item_code"], child["description"], child["uom"])
   190→
   191→        bom_item = {
   192→            "item_code": child["item_code"],
   193→            "qty": child["qty"],
   194→            "uom": child["uom"],
   195→            "custom_position": child["position"],
   196→            "custom_revision_no": child["revision"],
   197→            "custom_material": child["material"],
   198→            "custom_part_number": child["part_number"],
   199→            "custom_weight": child["weight"],
   200→            "custom_manufacturer": child["manufacturer"],
   201→            "custom_tipo_trattamento": child["treatment"],
   202→            "custom_level_of_bom": 1  # Always 1 for direct children
   203→        }
   204→
   205→        # If child has its own BOM, link to it
   206→        if child["children"]:
   207→            child_bom = frappe.db.get_value("BOM", {
   208→                "item": child["item_code"],
   209→                "is_active": 1,
   210→                "is_default": 1
   211→            }, "name")
   212→            if child_bom:
   213→                bom_item["bom_no"] = child_bom
   214→
   215→        bom.append("items", bom_item)
   216→
   217→    bom.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
   218→    bom.submit()
   219→    
   220→    return True
   221→
