1→# Engineer to order+PLM Component Master - Architecture Context
     2→
     3→## Document Purpose
     4→This document explains the business context, architectural decisions, and design rationale for the **Project Component Master** system in Clevertech's ERP implementation. It serves as a reference for understanding WHY specific design choices were made.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Related Documents
     9→
    10→This is part of a complete documentation suite for the Project Component Master system:
    11→
    12→1. **[clevertech_context.md](../clevertech_context.md)** (This Document)
    13→   - Business context and workflow
    14→   - Architectural decisions and rationale
    15→   - Design evolution and trade-offs
    16→   - **Read this first** to understand the "WHY"
    17→
    18→2. **[docs/bom_upload_code_study.md](docs/bom_upload_code_study.md)**
    19→   - Analysis of existing BOM Upload code
    20→   - What can be reused vs. what needs enhancement
    21→   - Integration points for new functionality
    22→   - **Read this second** to understand current implementation
    23→
    24→3. **[docs/project_component_master_tech_specs.md](docs/project_component_master_tech_specs.md)**
    25→   - Complete DocType JSON definitions
    26→   - All function specifications with signatures
    27→   - Test cases and validation logic
    28→   - Implementation checklist
    29→   - **Use this for implementation** - contains all technical details
    30→
    31→**Recommended Reading Order:**
    32→- **Manager/Stakeholder:** Read Context doc only
    33→- **Developer:** Read all three in order (Context → Code Study → Tech Specs)
    34→- **QA/Tester:** Focus on Context doc + Test Cases section in Tech Specs
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Table of Contents
    39→1. [Business Context](#business-context)
    40→2. [Current System & Limitations](#current-system--limitations)
    41→3. [Core Requirements](#core-requirements)
    42→4. [Architectural Decisions](#architectural-decisions)
    43→5. [Design Evolution](#design-evolution)
    44→6. [Final Architecture](#final-architecture)
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## Business Context
    49→
    50→### Company Profile
    51→**Clevertech** is an engineer-to-order (ETO) manufacturer of packaging machines. Their business model is characterized by:
    52→- Custom machine design per customer order
    53→- Long lead times for component procurement
    54→- Complex multi-level Bill of Materials (BOM)
    55→- Continuous design evolution during project execution
    56→
    57→### Current Workflow
    58→
    59→#### 1. Design Phase
    60→- Machines are designed in **SolidWorks** (3D CAD)
    61→- Bill of Materials (BOM) is maintained in **PE2** (PLM system)
    62→- A typical machine has **~500 components** across multiple levels
    63→- Not all components are designed upfront
    64→
    65→#### 2. Phased Design Release
    66→**Day 1:**
    67→- Only 2-3 components finalized
    68→- These components may have 4-level deep BOMs
    69→- Procurement must start immediately due to long lead times
    70→
    71→**Day 30:**
    72→- 3 more components released
    73→- Some existing components may have design revisions
    74→- Procurement for initial components already in progress
    75→
    76→**Day 60+:**
    77→- Continuous component additions and revisions
    78→- Some components procured as "loose items" before design finalized
    79→
    80→#### 3. Current ERPNext Integration
    81→**Process:**
    82→1. Export BOM from PE2 to Excel
    83→2. Upload Excel to ERPNext via "BOM Upload" doctype
    84→3. System creates Item masters and BOM structures
    85→4. Material Requests (MR) raised from BOMs
    86→5. Standard procurement workflow: MR → RFQ → Supplier Quotation → Purchase Order
    87→
    88→**Problem:** This works for initial upload but breaks down on subsequent uploads when components change.
    89→
    90→---
    91→
    92→## Current System & Limitations
    93→
    94→### Existing BOM Upload Logic
    95→
    96→**What it does:**
    97→```python
    98→1. Parse Excel file (specific column mapping from PE2 export)
    99→2. Build hierarchical tree structure from "level" column
   100→3. Create Item masters if they don't exist
   101→4. Create BOMs recursively (bottom-up approach)
   102→5. Skip if BOM exists (simple existence check)
   103→```
   104→
   105→**Critical Limitations:**
   106→
   107→#### Limitation 1: Blind Skipping
   108→```python
   109→# Current logic
   110→existing = frappe.db.exists("BOM", {
   111→    "item": item_code,
   112→    "is_active": 1,
   113→    "is_default": 1
   114→})
   115→
   116→if existing:
   117→    return False  # Skip, no questions asked
   118→```
   119→
   120→**Problem:** If Motor-Assembly BOM changed from 3 child items to 5 child items, system silently skips it. Procurement happens with outdated BOM.
   121→
   122→#### Limitation 2: No Change Detection
   123→- No comparison of BOM structure
   124→- No alerting when components change
   125→- No tracking of what changed (added/removed/qty modified)
   126→
   127→#### Limitation 3: No Procurement Tracking
   128→- Can't see which components have procurement initiated
   129→- No prevention of duplicate procurement
   130→- No visibility into "loose items" that later get added to BOMs
   131→
   132→#### Limitation 4: No Version Management
   133→- When BOM changes, old BOM just replaced
   134→- No audit trail of versions
   135→- Procurement documents may reference obsolete BOMs
   136→
   137→#### Limitation 5: No Budget/Timeline Tracking
   138→- No way to set target costs per component
   139→- No target delivery dates
   140→- No rolled-up cost estimates for assemblies
   141→
   142→#### Limitation 6: Loose Item Gap
   143→**Scenario:**
   144→```
   145→Day 1: Bearing (long lead time) → Procure 100 units as "loose item"
   146→Day 30: Motor-Assembly BOM uploaded → Requires Bearing × 50 units
   147→       → System creates new Material Request for 50 units
   148→       → Result: 150 units procured (50 excess!)
   149→```
   150→
   151→No mechanism to track that Bearing was already procured outside BOM context.
   152→
   153→---
   154→
   155→## Core Requirements
   156→
   157→### Requirement 1: Incremental BOM Updates
   158→**Need:** Upload full BOM export monthly/weekly, but only create NEW components, skip unchanged, and ALERT on changes.
   159→
   160→**Business Driver:** Design evolves continuously. Can't manually track 500 components across 20 projects.
   161→
   162→### Requirement 2: Change Detection with Impact Analysis
   163→**Need:** When Motor-Gearbox BOM changes, system must:
   164→- Detect the change (compare structure)
   165→- Find all parent assemblies using Motor-Gearbox
   166→- Block creation of entire chain until resolved
   167→- Show user what changed (qty, new items, removed items)
   168→
   169→**Business Driver:** Cost rollups and procurement tracking break if parent BOMs created with wrong child structure.
   170→
   171→### Requirement 3: Loose Item Management
   172→**Need:** Track components procured before design finalized:
   173→- Allow procurement without BOM
   174→- When component added to BOM later, prevent duplicate procurement
   175→- Track quantity limits: `MAX(loose_qty, bom_qty)` not `SUM`
   176→
   177→**Business Driver:** Long lead items (6+ months) must be ordered before design complete. Common in custom machinery.
   178→
   179→### Requirement 4: Version Control
   180→**Need:** When Motor-Assembly BOM changes:
   181→- Create new version (not replace)
   182→- Keep old version for existing POs
   183→- Track procurement per version
   184→- Allow parallel procurement (v1 and v2 simultaneously)
   185→
   186→**Business Driver:** May have orders for old version in production while new version being quoted.
   187→
   188→### Requirement 5: Budget & Timeline Tracking
   189→**Need:**
   190→- Set target cost per component (designer estimate)
   191→- Auto-calculate rolled-up cost from child components
   192→- Allow manual override (for assembly labor, overhead)
   193→- Track actual procurement cost vs budget
   194→
   195→**Business Driver:** Custom machines quoted based on component cost estimates. Need variance tracking.
   196→
   197→### Requirement 6: Procurement Quantity Control
   198→**Need:** Hard limit on Material Request quantities:
   199→```
   200→If Bearing: loose_qty=100, bom_qty=150
   201→Then: total_limit = MAX(100, 150) = 150
   202→Block any MR that would cause total procurement > 150
   203→```
   204→
   205→**Business Driver:** Prevent over-procurement. Inventory carrying costs are high for custom components.
   206→
   207→### Requirement 7: Comprehensive Reporting
   208→**Need:** Report showing per project:
   209→- All components (assemblies + loose items)
   210→- BOM hierarchy (levels)
   211→- Budget vs actual
   212→- Procurement status (Not Started / In Progress / Complete)
   213→- Linked documents (MR, RFQ, PO)
   214→
   215→**Business Driver:** Management needs project-level procurement visibility for cash flow planning.
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## Architectural Decisions
   220→
   221→### Decision 1: Introduce "Project Component Master" DocType
   222→
   223→**Problem:** ERPNext's native Item and BOM doctypes are project-agnostic. Can't track project-specific:
   224→- Procurement status per project
   225→- Budget per project (same bearing may have different target cost in different projects)
   226→- Loose item tracking per project
   227→
   228→**Solution:** New master doctype bridging Project ↔ Item ↔ BOM
   229→
   230→**Why not use existing doctypes?**
   231→
   232→| Option | Why Rejected |
   233→|--------|--------------|
   234→| **Custom fields on Item** | Items are global; same item used across projects with different budgets/status |
   235→| **Custom fields on BOM** | BOMs are global; same BOM may be used in multiple projects |
   236→| **Project doctype child table** | Can't handle deep hierarchy; poor query performance; limited customization |
   237→| **New DocType** | ✓ Project-specific context, ✓ Rich data model, ✓ Separate permissions |
   238→
   239→**Trade-off Accepted:** Additional complexity of maintaining Component Master, but gains flexibility and clarity.
   240→
   241→---
   242→
   243→### Decision 2: Component Master Scope - "Managed Components Only"
   244→
   245→**Options Considered:**
   246→
   247→**Option A: All Items**
   248→- Every item in every BOM gets Component Master entry
   249→- Including bolts, washers, raw materials
   250→
   251→**Rejected because:**
   252→- 500 components × 4 levels = 2000+ entries per project
   253→- 90% are standard catalog items (bolts, bearings)
   254→- No value in tracking procurement status for M6 bolt
   255→
   256→**Option B: Only Assemblies with BOMs**
   257→- Component Master for items that have their own BOM
   258→- Leaf items (raw materials) excluded
   259→
   260→**Rejected because:**
   261→- Loose items are often raw materials (bearing, shaft stock)
   262→- Would have nowhere to track loose procurement
   263→
   264→**Option C: Assemblies + Loose Items (CHOSEN)**
   265→```
   266→Project Component Master contains:
   267→✓ Assemblies/sub-assemblies (has_bom = Yes)
   268→✓ Loose raw materials (is_loose_item = Yes)
   269→✗ Regular leaf items in BOMs (just in BOM Item child table)
   270→```
   271→
   272→**Rationale:**
   273→- Focus on "managed components" - items requiring procurement oversight
   274→- Standard catalog items flow through normal BOM → MR process
   275→- Loose items get tracking even though they're raw materials
   276→- Keeps Component Master size manageable (~50-100 entries per project vs 2000+)
   277→
   278→---
   279→
   280→### Decision 3: Hierarchical Structure (Parent-Child)
   281→
   282→**Options Considered:**
   283→
   284→**Option A: Flat List**
   285→```
   286→Component Master:
   287→- Motor-Assembly (no parent link)
   288→- Gearbox-Sub (no parent link)
   289→- Shaft-001 (no parent link)
   290→```
   291→
   292→**Option B: Hierarchical**
   293→```
   294→Component Master:
   295→- Motor-Assembly (parent=null, level=0)
   296→- Gearbox-Sub (parent=Motor-Assembly, level=1)
   297→- Custom-Bracket (parent=Gearbox-Sub, level=2)
   298→```
   299→
   300→**Chosen: Option B (Hierarchical)**
   301→
   302→**Rationale:**
   303→1. **Cost Rollup:** Need to calculate Motor-Assembly cost = Gearbox cost + Housing cost + ...
   304→2. **Dependency Tracking:** If Gearbox changes, must know Motor-Assembly affected
   305→3. **Reporting:** Show "which assemblies are blocked by Gearbox delay?"
   306→4. **Business Context:** PE2 export has hierarchy (level column), natural to preserve it
   307→
   308→**Implementation Detail:**
   309→- Hierarchy implicit in PE2 export (level 0, 1, 2...)
   310→- BOM Upload derives parent-child during tree building
   311→- Auto-populates `parent_component` and `bom_level` fields
   312→
   313→---
   314→
   315→### Decision 3A: BOM Versioning and Change Propagation
   316→
   317→**CRITICAL UNDERSTANDING: BOMs are Version-Locked**
   318→
   319→**How ERPNext BOMs Work:**
   320→```
   321→Parent BOMs reference children by ITEM CODE with VERSION FROZEN at submission:
   322→
   323→BOM-A-001 (submitted):
   324→  Items:
   325→    - Item: B (qty: 1)  → Uses BOM-B-001 (frozen)
   326→
   327→BOM-C-001 (submitted):
   328→  Items:
   329→    - Item: B (qty: 3)  → Uses BOM-B-001 (frozen)
   330→```
   331→
   332→**When Child BOM Changes:**
   333→
   334→**Scenario:** Design team updates item B's structure in SolidWorks → PE2 → New Excel export
   335→
   336→**What Happens:**
   337→1. New Excel file contains:
   338→   - Updated BOM for item B
   339→   - BOM for item A (which uses B)
   340→   - BOM for item C (which uses B)
   341→
   342→2. BOM Upload creates NEW versions:
   343→   - BOM-B-002 (new structure for B)
   344→   - BOM-A-002 (uses BOM-B-002)
   345→   - BOM-C-002 (uses BOM-B-002)
   346→
   347→3. Old versions become inactive:
   348→   - BOM-A-001 still exists but inactive
   349→   - BOM-B-001 still exists but inactive
   350→   - BOM-C-001 still exists but inactive
   351→
   352→**Key Insight:**
   353→- Parent BOMs **DO NOT** automatically use new child BOM versions
   354→- BOMs are **IMMUTABLE** after submission
   355→- Change propagates via **complete BOM Upload** (all affected BOMs created with new versions)
   356→- This is why PE2 exports the ENTIRE hierarchy (not just changed items)
   357→
   358→**Impact on Component Master:**
   359→
   360→```
   361→Before BOM Upload (Old State):
   362→Component Master for B:
   363→├── active_bom = BOM-B-001
   364→├── BOM Usage Table:
   365→    ├── parent_bom = BOM-A-001
   366→    └── parent_bom = BOM-C-001
   367→
   368→After BOM Upload (New State):
   369→Component Master for B:
   370→├── active_bom = BOM-B-002  ← UPDATED
   371→├── BOM Usage Table:
   372→    ├── parent_bom = BOM-A-002  ← UPDATED (old row removed, new row added)
   373→    └── parent_bom = BOM-C-002  ← UPDATED (old row removed, new row added)
   374→```
   375→
   376→**Blocking Logic:**
   377→```
   378→When BOM Upload attempts to create BOM-B-002:
   379→
   380→1. Check: Does Component Master for B exist? → YES
   381→2. Check: Does B have active procurement? → Check procurement_records table
   382→3. If YES (procurement exists):
   383→   - BLOCK entire BOM Upload
   384→   - Prevents creation of BOM-A-002, BOM-B-002, BOM-C-002
   385→   - Show user: "Item B has procurement. Cannot change. Affects BOM-A-001, BOM-C-001"
   386→   - User must resolve/cancel procurement before uploading new file
   387→
   388→4. If NO (no procurement):
   389→   - Allow BOM Upload
   390→   - Create BOM-A-002, BOM-B-002, BOM-C-002
   391→   - Deactivate BOM-A-001, BOM-B-001, BOM-C-001
   392→   - Update Component Master BOM Usage tables
   393→```
   394→
   395→**Why This Matters:**
   396→1. **Procurement Control:** A single procured item blocks entire hierarchy update
   397→2. **Change Traceability:** BOM versions track exact configuration at point in time
   398→3. **Surgical Blocking:** Only affected items with procurement block upload
   399→4. **Unaffected Siblings:** If item D (not in hierarchy) has procurement, it doesn't block A/B/C changes
   400→
   401→**Rationale:**
   402→- ERPNext's BOM immutability prevents mid-procurement confusion
   403→- PE2's full hierarchy export ensures all parent references update together
   404→- Component Master tracks current active versions + parent-child relationships
   405→- Blocking logic prevents breaking existing procurement commitments
   406→
   407→---
   408→
   409→### Decision 4: Loose Item Conversion Logic
   410→
   411→**Key Insight:** Loose items are **always raw materials**, never become assemblies with BOMs.
   412→
   413→**Status States (Based on Actual BOM Usage):**
   414→```
   415→State 1: Pending Conversion
   416→- is_loose_item = Yes
   417→- has_bom = No
   418→- bom_conversion_status = Pending Conversion
   419→- bom_usage table is empty (not used in any BOM yet)
   420→
   421→State 2: Converted to BOM
   422→- is_loose_item = Yes (preserves history)
   423→- has_bom = No (still a raw material)
   424→- bom_conversion_status = Converted to BOM
   425→- bom_usage table has 1 entry (used in exactly one BOM)
   426→
   427→State 3: Partial (Used in Multiple BOMs)
   428→- is_loose_item = Yes
   429→- has_bom = No
   430→- bom_conversion_status = Partial
   431→- bom_usage table has 2+ entries (used in multiple BOMs)
   432→```
   433→
   434→**Important:** Status is determined by **actual BOM usage**, not by the checkbox.
   435→
   436→**Permission Gate (Separate from Status):**
   437→```
   438→can_be_converted_to_bom (checkbox):
   439→- Default: No (unchecked)
   440→- Purpose: Permission gate to control BOM Upload
   441→- Does NOT change status
   442→- If No: BOM Upload BLOCKED (hard error)
   443→- If Yes: BOM Upload ALLOWED (status updates when actually used)
   444→```
   445→
   446→**Blocking Logic:**
   447→```python
   448→if is_loose_item and not can_be_converted_to_bom:
   449→    # HARD BLOCK - cannot use in any BOM
   450→    raise Exception("Enable conversion first")
   451→```
   452→
   453→**Why keep `is_loose_item=Yes` even after conversion?**
   454→- Preserves procurement history
   455→- Loose vs BOM procurement tracked separately
   456→- Helps explain why total_qty_limit might be higher than BOM requirement
   457→
   458→**Rationale:** Designer must explicitly confirm "design finalized, OK to use in production BOM" by checking the box, but status only changes when item is actually added to a BOM.
   459→
   460→---
   461→
   462→### Decision 5: Procurement Quantity Control - MAX not SUM
   463→
   464→**Critical Business Logic:**
   465→
   466→**Scenario:**
   467→```
   468→Bearing-SKF-001:
   469→- Day 1: Loose procurement = 100 units (long lead time)
   470→- Day 30: Motor-Assembly BOM = 50 units
   471→- Day 45: Pump-Assembly BOM = 30 units
   472→- Total BOM requirement: 80 units
   473→```
   474→
   475→**Question:** What's the procurement limit?
   476→
   477→**Wrong Answer: SUM**
   478→```
   479→Total limit = 100 (loose) + 80 (BOM) = 180
   480→Problem: Bearing procured twice! 100 loose + 80 from BOM = 180 units
   481→But only need MAX(100, 80) = 100 units
   482→```
   483→
   484→**Correct Answer: MAX**
   485→```
   486→Total limit = MAX(100, 80) = 100 units
   487→Rationale:
   488→- If loose=100 and BOM=80: Loose already covers BOM, no additional procurement
   489→- If loose=100 and BOM=150: Need 150 total, loose covers 100, procure 50 more
   490→```
   491→
   492→**Implementation:**
   493→```python
   494→total_qty_limit = MAX(loose_qty_required, bom_qty_required)
   495→
   496→# Material Request Validation
   497→if total_procurement_attempt > total_qty_limit:
   498→    raise Exception("Exceeds limit")
   499→```
   500→
   501→**Edge Case Handling:**
   502→```
   503→If loose=200, BOM=100:
   504→- total_limit = MAX(200, 100) = 200 ✓
   505→- But BOM MR should be 0 (already over-procured!)
   506→- System shows: "Warning: Loose procurement (200) exceeds BOM (100)"
   507→- Allows MR up to 200 total, not 200 + 100
   508→```
   509→
   510→---
   511→
   512→### Decision 6: Change Detection - Hash-Based Comparison
   513→
   514→**Problem:** How to detect if BOM structure changed?
   515→
   516→**Options Considered:**
   517→
   518→**Option A: Field-by-Field Comparison**
   519→```python
   520→for each child:
   521→    compare item_code, qty, uom, custom_fields...
   522→```
   523→Rejected: Slow, complex, miss changes if new field added
   524→
   525→**Option B: BOM Version Number**
   526→```python
   527→if old_bom.version != new_bom.version:
   528→    changed = True
   529→```
   530→Rejected: PE2 doesn't export version cleanly, prone to false positives
   531→
   532→**Option C: Structure Hash (CHOSEN)**
   533→```python
   534→structure = sorted([(item_code, qty) for child in children])
   535→hash = md5(str(structure))
   536→```
   537→
   538→**Rationale:**
   539→- Fast comparison (single string match)
   540→- Captures structural changes (items added/removed/qty changed)
   541→- Ignores non-structural changes (descriptions, custom fields)
   542→- Same approach used by existing duplicate detection validation
   543→
   544→**What triggers "changed"?**
   545→- ✓ Child item added
   546→- ✓ Child item removed
   547→- ✓ Child item quantity changed
   548→- ✗ Child item description changed (not structural)
   549→- ✗ Custom fields changed (position, revision, material)
   550→
   551→**Trade-off:** May miss "soft" changes (revision updated but BOM structure same). Acceptable because revision tracked separately in BOM Item custom fields.
   552→
   553→---
   554→
   555→### Decision 7: Change Blocking Strategy - Ancestor Chain
   556→
   557→**Problem:** When Gearbox BOM changes, what should be blocked?
   558→
   559→**Options Considered:**
   560→
   561→**Option A: Block Entire Upload (All or Nothing)**
   562→```
   563→If ANY component changed → Block ALL components
   564→```
   565→Rejected: Unnecessarily delays 98 components because 2 changed
   566→
   567→**Option B: Block Only Changed Component**
   568→```
   569→Block: Gearbox
   570→Allow: Motor-Assembly (uses Gearbox)
   571→```
   572→Rejected: Creates Motor-Assembly BOM with wrong Gearbox structure
   573→
   574→**Option C: Block Ancestor Chain (CHOSEN)**
   575→```
   576→If Gearbox changed:
   577→- Block Gearbox ✓
   578→- Block Motor-Assembly (uses Gearbox) ✓
   579→- Block Main-Machine (uses Motor-Assembly) ✓
   580→- Allow Pump-Assembly (doesn't use Gearbox) ✓
   581→```
   582→
   583→**Algorithm:**
   584→```python
   585→1. Identify changed components
   586→2. For each changed component:
   587→   - Find all ancestors (components containing it, recursively)
   588→   - Add ancestors to blocked set
   589→3. Allow creation of components NOT in blocked set
   590→```
   591→
   592→**Why this approach?**
   593→- **Surgical:** Only blocks what's affected
   594→- **Safe:** Preserves BOM integrity (no partial structures)
   595→- **Efficient:** Allows parallel work on unrelated components
   596→
   597→**Multi-Level Example:**
   598→```
   599→Upload contains:
   600→- Machine
   601→  ├── Module-A
   602→  │   └── Assembly-X (unchanged)
   603→  └── Module-B
   604→      └── Gearbox (CHANGED)
   605→
   606→Result:
   607→✓ Allow: Assembly-X (no dependency on Gearbox)
   608→✗ Block: Gearbox (changed itself)
   609→✗ Block: Module-B (contains Gearbox)
   610→✗ Block: Machine (contains Module-B which contains Gearbox)
   611→```
   612→
   613→---
   614→
   615→### Decision 8: Budget Tracking - No Child Table
   616→
   617→**Problem:** Need to track budget at component level AND leaf item level for cost rollup.
   618→
   619→**Options Considered:**
   620→
   621→**Option A: Budget Child Table in Component Master**
   622→```
   623→Project Component Master: Motor-Assembly
   624→├── budgeted_rate: $500 (parent level)
   625→└── Child Table: Item Budgets
   626→    ├── Shaft: $100
   627→    ├── Bearing: $50
   628→    └── Housing: $200
   629→```
   630→
   631→**Rejected because:**
   632→- Duplication: BOM already has item list
   633→- Sync issues: If BOM changes, budget table out of sync
   634→- Maintenance burden: Manual entry for every child item
   635→- Complexity: What if child item also has BOM? Recursive budgets?
   636→
   637→**Option B: Budget in BOM Item (ERPNext child table)**
   638→```
   639→BOM: Motor-Assembly
   640→└── BOM Items:
   641→    ├── Shaft, qty=1, custom_budgeted_rate=$100
   642→    ├── Bearing, qty=2, custom_budgeted_rate=$50
   643→```
   644→
   645→**Rejected because:**
   646→- Budget lives in BOM, not Component Master
   647→- If multiple BOM versions, which budget applies?
   648→- Hard to report "all component budgets" without querying BOMs
   649→
   650→**Option C: Budget Only for Managed Components (CHOSEN)**
   651→```
   652→Project Component Master:
   653→├── Motor-Assembly: budgeted_rate=$500 (manual entry)
   654→├── Gearbox-Sub: budgeted_rate=$300 (manual entry)
   655→└── Custom-Bracket: budgeted_rate=$150 (loose item)
   656→
   657→Rollup Logic:
   658→Motor-Assembly cost =
   659→  Gearbox-Sub budget ($300 from Component Master) +
   660→  Bearing actual ($120 from last PO) +
   661→  Bolts actual ($8 from last PO)
   662→```
   663→
   664→**Rationale:**
   665→- Budgets only for "managed components" (assemblies + loose items)
   666→- Leaf catalog items (bolts, bearings) use actual procurement cost
   667→- Designer estimates at assembly level, not bolt level
   668→- Rollup combines budgets (for managed) + actuals (for catalog items)
   669→
   670→**Business Context:**
   671→- ETO quoting done at assembly level ("Motor section: $5000")
   672→- Not at leaf level ("M6 bolt: $0.05")
   673→- This matches how designers think about costs
   674→
   675→---
   676→
   677→### Decision 9: Material Request Validation - Hard Block
   678→
   679→**Problem:** How to enforce procurement quantity limits?
   680→
   681→**Options Considered:**
   682→
   683→**Option A: Warning Only**
   684→```python
   685→if mr_qty > limit:
   686→    frappe.msgprint("Warning: Exceeds limit")
   687→    # But allow save
   688→```
   689→Rejected: Users ignore warnings, over-procurement happens
   690→
   691→**Option B: Auto-Adjust**
   692→```python
   693→if mr_qty > limit:
   694→    mr_qty = max_allowed
   695→    frappe.msgprint("Adjusted to maximum")
   696→```
   697→Rejected: Silent changes confusing, may hide data entry errors
   698→
   699→**Option C: Hard Block (CHOSEN)**
   700→```python
   701→if mr_qty > limit:
   702→    frappe.throw("Cannot exceed limit")
   703→    # Save fails
   704→```
   705→
   706→**Rationale:**
   707→- Over-procurement is costly (inventory carrying, obsolescence)
   708→- Better to force user decision than allow mistake
   709→- User can increase limit in Component Master if genuinely needed
   710→- Clear error message explains why and what limit is
   711→
   712→**UX Consideration:**
   713→Error message shows:
   714→```
   715→Cannot add 200 units of Bearing-SKF to Material Request
   716→
   717→Procurement Limit Exceeded:
   718→• Total Limit: 150 (from Component Master)
   719→• Existing MRs: 100
   720→• This MR: 200
   721→• Total: 300
   722→
   723→Maximum allowed for this MR: 50
   724→```
   725→
   726→Gives user actionable information to fix.
   727→
   728→---
   729→
   730→### Decision 10: BOM Versioning - Use ERPNext Native
   731→
   732→**Decision:** Don't build custom versioning, use ERPNext's native BOM versioning.
   733→
   734→**Rationale:**
   735→- ERPNext already supports multiple BOMs per item
   736→- Activate/deactivate mechanism exists
   737→- Reports and queries understand BOM status
   738→- Less code to maintain
   739→
   740→**Component Master Role:**
   741→- Tracks `active_bom` (current version)
   742→- Child table tracks BOM version history
   743→- Provides project-specific version context
   744→
   745→**When BOM changes:**
   746→```
   747→Old approach (custom): BOM-MOT-001-V1, BOM-MOT-001-V2
   748→New approach (native): BOM-MOT-001, BOM-MOT-002 (ERPNext auto-naming)
   749→
   750→Component Master:
   751→- active_bom = BOM-MOT-002
   752→- BOM Version History:
   753→  - BOM-MOT-001 (v1, created 2026-01-01, procurement: PO-001)
   754→  - BOM-MOT-002 (v2, created 2026-02-15, procurement: PO-045)
   755→```
   756→
   757→**User Workflow:**
   758→1. Upload detects change → Show dialog
   759→2. User manually creates new BOM (or lets system create with new name)
   760→3. User deactivates old BOM
   761→4. System updates Component Master.active_bom
   762→
   763→---
   764→
   765→### Decision 11: Two Distinct Flows for Component Master Creation
   766→
   767→**CRITICAL: Component Masters are created differently depending on the scenario.**
   768→
   769→**Flow 1: Cutover (Existing Projects)**
   770→```
   771→Projects where BOMs already exist in ERPNext (e.g., SMR260001 with 24 BOMs).
   772→
   773→Sequence:
   774→1. BOMs already exist (submitted, active, default)
   775→2. User clicks "Generate Component Masters" on Project form
   776→3. System creates Component Masters FROM existing BOMs
   777→4. Sets: has_bom=1, active_bom=BOM-xxx, bom_structure_hash, design_status="Design Released"
   778→5. Populates BOM Usage tables retroactively
   779→
   780→Direction: BOM → Component Master (retrospective)
   781→Implementation: Phase 2A — bulk_generation.py + project.js
   782→```
   783→
   784→**Flow 2: New Projects (Ongoing PE2 Uploads)**
   785→```
   786→New project, first PE2 export uploaded to BOM Upload doctype.
   787→
   788→Sequence:
   789→1. Parse PE2 Excel → build tree of assemblies
   790→2. Create Component Masters FIRST (has_bom=1, active_bom=null)
   791→3. Run analysis — check procurement blocks, loose items, changes
   792→4. Create BOMs bottom-up (existing BOM Upload logic)
   793→5. Link back — set active_bom and bom_structure_hash on Component Masters
   794→6. BOM on_submit hooks fire → populate BOM Usage tables automatically
   795→
   796→Direction: Component Master → BOM (proactive)
   797→Implementation: Phase 3 — enhanced BOM Upload
   798→```
   799→
   800→**Why Component Masters BEFORE BOMs in Flow 2:**
   801→- **Blocking logic needs them:** During BOM creation, the system checks if a component has active procurement or is a loose item without conversion enabled. These checks require the Component Master to already exist.
   802→- **Analysis requires them:** The analyze_upload() function queries Component Masters to categorize items as new/unchanged/changed/blocked.
   803→- **active_bom is set later:** Component Master is created with active_bom=null, then updated after BOM is successfully created and submitted.
   804→
   805→**Why BOMs BEFORE Component Masters in Flow 1:**
   806→- **No analysis needed:** Cutover data is already clean — BOMs are submitted and active.
   807→- **No blocking possible:** No procurement exists yet for these items.
   808→- **Simpler:** Just read existing BOMs and create tracking records.
   809→
   810→**Incremental Upload (Day 30, Day 60...):**
   811→```
   812→Subsequent PE2 uploads for an existing project follow Flow 2:
   813→
   814→1. Parse Excel → build tree
   815→2. Some items already have Component Masters (from previous upload)
   816→3. New items: Create Component Masters first, then BOMs
   817→4. Unchanged items: Skip (hash comparison)
   818→5. Changed items: Block ancestor chain, require resolution
   819→6. BOM hooks update existing Component Masters automatically
   820→```
   821→
   822→**Rationale:**
   823→- Cutover is a one-time operation, simplicity wins
   824→- Ongoing uploads need full validation pipeline, safety wins
   825→- Both flows produce the same end state: Component Master with active_bom linked
   826→
   827→---
   828→
   829→### Decision 12: New Button on BOM Upload — Reuse Existing Code via Imports
   830→
   831→**CRITICAL: Do NOT modify the existing BOM Upload code written by another developer.**
   832→
   833→**Strategy: Option B — New button, import their functions**
   834→
   835→**Existing BOM Upload code** (`clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload.py`) provides:
   836→```
   837→Importable Functions:
   838→├── parse_rows(ws)                              → Excel worksheet → list of row dicts
   839→├── build_tree(rows)                            → Row list → nested tree (stack-based, uses "level" field)
   840→├── ensure_item_exists(item_code, desc, uom)    → Create Item master if missing
   841→├── create_bom_recursive(node, project, root)   → Bottom-up BOM creation + child linking
   842→└── create_boms(docname)                        → Main entry point (existing button calls this)
   843→
   844→Helper Functions:
   845→├── clean_code(code)                            → Remove dots, strip whitespace from item codes
   846→├── to_float(val, default)                      → Safe float conversion
   847→└── normalize_uom(uom)                          → NUMERI→Nos, PEZZI→Nos, METRI→Meter
   848→```
   849→
   850→**Key finding:** Item creation happens INSIDE `create_bom_recursive()` — it calls
   851→`ensure_item_exists()` for each node before creating the BOM. Item + BOM creation
   852→are interleaved, not separate phases.
   853→
   854→**Our new button's sequence (using their imports):**
   855→```
   856→Step 1: parse_rows(ws)                          → Their code — parse Excel
   857→Step 2: build_tree(rows)                        → Their code — build hierarchy
   858→Step 3: Walk tree → ensure_item_exists()        → Their code — Items must exist before
   859→         for all assembly nodes                   Component Masters (Link field dependency)
   860→Step 4: Create Component Masters                → OUR code — assemblies only, active_bom=null
   861→Step 5: Run analysis                            → OUR code — hash comparison, blocking checks
   862→Step 6: create_bom_recursive()                  → Their code — bottom-up BOM creation
   863→         (calls ensure_item_exists again,          (harmless — checks existence, returns early)
   864→          for leaf items too)
   865→Step 7: Link active_bom back to                 → OUR code — set active_bom + hash
   866→         Component Masters
   867→```
   868→
   869→**Dependency chain:**
   870→```
   871→Items must exist       → before Component Masters  (item_code is Link field to Item)
   872→Component Masters must → before analysis           (blocking checks query Component Master)
   873→exist
   874→Analysis must pass     → before BOM creation        (blocked = stop, don't create BOMs)
   875→BOMs must exist        → before linking active_bom   (need BOM name to set on Component Master)
   876→```
   877→
   878→**Why this works without touching their code:**
   879→- Their existing "Create BOMs" button continues to work as-is
   880→- Our new "Create BOMs with Validation" button imports their functions
   881→- `ensure_item_exists()` is idempotent (checks first, skips if exists)
   882→- `create_bom_recursive()` is idempotent (skips if BOM already active/default)
   883→- Users choose which button to use: old (no tracking) or new (with Component Masters)
   884→
   885→---
   886→
   887→### Decision 13: Project Quantity Multiplication for bom_qty_required
   888→
   889→**Problem Identified:** `bom_qty_required` was summing raw `qty_per_unit` from BOM Usage table without accounting for how many units of the parent assembly the project actually needs.
   890→
   891→**Example:**
   892→```
   893→BOM-A (project needs 2 units)
   894→├── RM-1 (qty_per_unit = 2)
   895→└── RM-2 (qty_per_unit = 5)
   896→
   897→Wrong (current):   bom_qty_required for RM-1 = 2   (just qty_per_unit)
   898→Correct:           bom_qty_required for RM-1 = 4   (2 × 2 = project_qty × qty_per_unit)
   899→                   bom_qty_required for RM-2 = 10  (2 × 5)
   900→```
   901→
   902→**Nested Example:**
   903→```
   904→TOP-ASSY (project_qty = 3)
   905→├── SUB-ASSY (qty_per_unit = 2, project_qty = 3 × 2 = 6)
   906→│   └── RM-X (qty_per_unit = 4, total_qty_required = 6 × 4 = 24)
   907→└── RM-Y (qty_per_unit = 10, total_qty_required = 3 × 10 = 30)
   908→```
   909→
   910→**New field: `project_qty`** on Project Component Master
   911→- Stores: "How many units of this component does the project need?"
   912→- For root assemblies: comes from Excel column E (qty field)
   913→- For sub-assemblies: `parent_project_qty × qty_per_unit_in_parent`
   914→- For raw materials: not directly stored here — derived via BOM Usage table
   915→
   916→**Corrected calculation:**
   917→```python
   918→def calculate_bom_qty_required(self):
   919→    total = 0
   920→    for usage in self.bom_usage:
   921→        parent_project_qty = lookup parent assembly's project_qty from its Component Master
   922→        usage.total_qty_required = usage.qty_per_unit × parent_project_qty
   923→        total += usage.total_qty_required
   924→    self.bom_qty_required = total
   925→```
   926→
   927→**Two flows handle project_qty differently:**
   928→
   929→| Flow | Source of project_qty | Mechanism |
   930→|------|----------------------|-----------|
   931→| **BOM Upload** (Flow 2) | Excel column E — parsed by `parse_rows()`, cascaded top-down through tree | Auto-set by `bom_upload_enhanced.py` after BOM creation. All nodes (assemblies + raw materials) covered. |
   932→| **Cutover** (Flow 1) | No Excel available — BOMs already exist with `quantity: 1` | User must manually enter `project_qty` on root assembly CMs. Defaults to 1. See cutover limitations below. |
   933→
   934→**Cutover Flow Limitations (bulk_generation.py):**
   935→```
   936→What cutover creates:
   937→  TOP-ASSY (has BOM → gets CM, project_qty = 1 default)
   938→  ├── SUB-ASSY (has BOM → gets CM, project_qty = 1 default)
   939→  │   └── RM-1 (no BOM → NO CM created by cutover)
   940→  └── RM-2 (no BOM → NO CM created by cutover)
   941→
   942→Key gaps:
   943→1. Root assembly has NO bom_usage rows (nothing references it as a child)
   944→   → Its project_qty has no automatic source — must be manually entered
   945→2. Raw materials (leaf nodes) have NO Component Master from cutover
   946→   → bom_usage rows for them are silently skipped by add_or_update_bom_usage()
   947→   → Even if project_qty is set on parent assemblies, raw materials
   948→     can't compute bom_qty_required without their own CM + bom_usage rows
   949→3. The entire multiplication chain depends on root assembly's project_qty
   950→   → If user doesn't set it, everything defaults to 1 (safe but incomplete)
   951→
   952→Consequence: For cutover data, procurement qty limits only work correctly after:
   953→  a) User manually enters project_qty on root assembly CM
   954→  b) Raw materials get CMs created (via loose item flow or future enhancement)
   955→  c) Component Masters are saved to trigger before_save() recalculation
   956→```
   957→
   958→**Impact on Decision 5 (MAX logic):**
   959→Decision 5's example `bom_qty_required: 80` (50 + 30) implicitly assumed project_qty=1 for both assemblies. With project_qty multiplication:
   960→```
   961→If Motor-Assembly project_qty=2, Pump-Assembly project_qty=1:
   962→  Bearing BOM requirement = (50 × 2) + (30 × 1) = 130
   963→  total_qty_limit = MAX(loose=100, bom=130) = 130
   964→```
   965→
   966→**Files to modify:**
   967→- `project_component_master.json` — add `project_qty` field (editable, not read-only)
   968→- `project_component_master.py` — fix `calculate_bom_qty_required()` to use multiplication
   969→- `bom_upload_enhanced.py` — walk tree top-down and set `project_qty` after upload
   970→- `bulk_generation.py` — set `project_qty = 1` as default for cutover (user can edit later)
   971→
   972→---
   973→
   974→## Design Evolution
   975→
   976→This section documents key design iterations during the architecture discussion.
   977→
   978→### Evolution 1: Component Master Scope
   979→
   980→**Initial Proposal:**
   981→> "Create Component Master only for items having BOM (excluding loose items)"
   982→
   983→**Issue Identified:**
   984→> "But loose items have no BOM, where do they live?"
   985→
   986→**Resolution:**
   987→Changed scope to: `has_bom = Yes OR is_loose_item = Yes`
   988→
   989→**Learning:** Loose items are a special case requiring tracking despite being leaf nodes.
   990→
   991→---
   992→
   993→### Evolution 2: Procurement Quantity Logic
   994→
   995→**Initial Proposal:**
   996→> "If loose=100 and BOM=50, error if MR qty > 100"
   997→
   998→**Issue Identified:**
   999→> "What if loose=100 and BOM=150? Using MAX(100, 150)=150 is correct, but what about the 100 already procured?"
  1000→
  1001→**Resolution:**
  1002→- total_qty_limit = MAX(loose, bom)
  1003→- total_procurement = SUM(all MRs including loose)
  1004→- Block if: total_procurement > total_qty_limit
  1005→- Warning if: loose > bom (over-procurement alert)
  1006→
  1007→**Learning:** Need to track cumulative procurement across loose + BOM sources.
  1008→
  1009→---
  1010→
  1011→### Evolution 3: Change Blocking Granularity
  1012→
  1013→**Initial Proposal:**
  1014→> "Block entire upload until changes resolved"
  1015→
  1016→**Issue Identified:**
  1017→> "If 100 components uploaded and 2 changed, blocks 98 good components unnecessarily"
  1018→
  1019→**Resolution:**
  1020→Staged approach:
  1021→1. Categorize: new / unchanged / changed / blocked
  1022→2. Create new and unchanged immediately
  1023→3. Block only components dependent on changed items
  1024→4. Show resolution dialog for changed items
  1025→
  1026→**Learning:** Surgical blocking maintains efficiency while ensuring safety.
  1027→
  1028→---
  1029→
  1030→### Evolution 4: Loose Item State Model
  1031→
  1032→**Initial Proposal:**
  1033→> "is_loose_item becomes No after conversion"
  1034→
  1035→**Issue Identified:**
  1036→> "Loses procurement history and can't explain why total_limit > bom_qty"
  1037→
  1038→**Resolution:**
  1039→- Keep `is_loose_item = Yes` permanently
  1040→- Add `bom_conversion_status` field (Pending / Converted / Partial)
  1041→- Loose vs BOM procurement tracked separately in child table
  1042→
  1043→**Learning:** State transitions, not binary flags, better model complex workflows.
  1044→
  1045→---
  1046→
  1047→### Evolution 5: Budget Tracking Complexity
  1048→
  1049→**Initial Proposal:**
  1050→> "Budget at component level and leaf level in child table"
  1051→
  1052→**Issue Identified:**
  1053→> "Child table duplicates BOM structure, sync nightmare, high maintenance"
  1054→
  1055→**Resolution:**
  1056→- Budget only for managed components (assemblies + loose)
  1057→- Rollup uses: managed component budgets + leaf item actual costs
  1058→- Matches business practice (designers estimate at assembly level)
  1059→
  1060→**Learning:** Simplicity over completeness when complexity doesn't add business value.
  1061→
  1062→---
  1063→
  1064→## Final Architecture
  1065→
  1066→### System Components
  1067→
  1068→```
  1069→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1070→│                     BOM Upload (Excel)                       │
  1071→│                  (PE2 Export, ~500 rows)                     │
  1072→└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────┘
  1073→                            │
  1074→                            ▼
  1075→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  1076→│              BOM Upload Analysis Engine                      │
  1077→│  Step 1: Parse Excel → Build tree                            │
  1078→│  Step 2: Create Component Masters FIRST (active_bom=null)    │
  1079→│  Step 3: Analyze — compare hashes, check procurement blocks  │
  1080→│  Step 4: Categorize: new / unchanged / changed / blocked     │
  1081→└───────────┬─────────────────────────────────┬───────────────┘
  1082→            │                                 │
  1083→            │ If blocked/changed              │ If clear
  1084→            ▼                                 ▼
  1085→┌─────────────────────────┐   ┌──────────────────────────────┐
  1086→│ Change Resolution Dialog│   │  Create BOMs (bottom-up)     │
  1087→│  • Show changes          │   │  • Use existing BOM Upload    │
  1088→│  • List blocked items    │   │    logic for BOM creation    │
  1089→│  • Require user action   │   │  • Link active_bom back to   │
  1090→│                          │   │    Component Masters         │
  1091→└─────────────────────────┘   │  • BOM hooks auto-populate    │
  1092→                               │    BOM Usage tables           │
  1093→                               └────────────┬─────────────────┘
  1094→                                            │
  1095→                                            ▼
  1096→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1097→                    │   Project Component Master            │
  1098→                    │   • Tracks managed components         │
  1099→                    │   • Budget & timeline                 │
  1100→                    │   • Procurement status                │
  1101→                    │   • Quantity limits                   │
  1102→                    └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘
  1103→                                │
  1104→                                ▼
  1105→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1106→                    │   Material Request Validation         │
  1107→                    │   • Check quantity limits             │
  1108→                    │   • Enforce MAX(loose, bom)           │
  1109→                    │   • Hard block if exceeded            │
  1110→                    └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘
  1111→                                │
  1112→                                ▼
  1113→                    ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐
  1114→                    │   Standard ERPNext Procurement        │
  1115→                    │   MR → RFQ → Quote → PO → Receipt     │
  1116→                    └───────────────────────────────────────┘
  1117→```
  1118→
  1119→### Data Flow
  1120→
  1121→**Scenario: Initial Upload (Day 1) — New Project via PE2**
  1122→```
  1123→1. Upload BOM Excel → 2 assemblies, 10 leaf items
  1124→2. Create Items for assemblies (ensure_item_exists — idempotent):
  1125→   - 2 Item masters for assembly nodes (Link field dependency)
  1126→3. Create Component Masters:
  1127→   - 2 Component Master entries (assemblies only)
  1128→   - has_bom=1, active_bom=null, design_status="Design Released"
  1129→4. Analysis: All new, no blocking issues
  1130→5. Create BOMs bottom-up (create_bom_recursive):
  1131→   - 2 BOM structures (with 10 leaf items in BOM Items)
  1132→   - 10 Item masters for leaf items (ensure_item_exists called again, idempotent)
  1133→6. Link back: Set active_bom and bom_structure_hash on Component Masters
  1134→7. BOM on_submit hooks populate BOM Usage tables
  1135→8. Final state: Design Released, Procurement Not Started
  1136→```
  1137→
  1138→**Scenario: Loose Item Procurement (Day 5)**
  1139→```
  1140→1. User manually creates Component Master entry
  1141→   - item_code: Bearing-SKF-001
  1142→   - is_loose_item: Yes
  1143→   - loose_qty_required: 100
  1144→   - loose_item_reason: "Long lead time, design pending"
  1145→2. Create Material Request: 100 units
  1146→3. Component Master auto-updates:
  1147→   - total_qty_limit: 100
  1148→   - total_qty_procured: 100
  1149→   - procurement_status: In Progress
  1150→```
  1151→
  1152→**Scenario: Incremental Upload (Day 30)**
  1153→```
  1154→1. Upload BOM Excel → 5 assemblies total
  1155→2. Analysis:
  1156→   - 2 unchanged (skip)
  1157→   - 2 new (create)
  1158→   - 1 changed (Gearbox structure modified)
  1159→3. Check dependencies:
  1160→   - Motor-Assembly uses Gearbox → Block Motor
  1161→   - Pump-Assembly doesn't use Gearbox → Allow
  1162→4. Show dialog:
  1163→   - "Gearbox changed, blocks Motor-Assembly"
  1164→   - "Create 2 new components (Pump-Assembly, Valve-Block)?"
  1165→5. User resolves Gearbox change (deactivates old BOM)
  1166→6. Re-run upload → Creates all remaining components
  1167→```
  1168→
  1169→**Scenario: Material Request with Limit Check (Day 35)**
  1170→```
  1171→1. User creates MR from Motor-Assembly BOM
  1172→2. Includes Bearing-SKF-001 (qty 50)
  1173→3. Validation hook:
  1174→   - Checks Component Master
  1175→   - loose_qty_required: 100
  1176→   - bom_qty_required: 50 (from Motor BOM)
  1177→   - total_qty_limit: MAX(100, 50) = 100
  1178→   - Existing procurement: 100 (loose MR)
  1179→   - This MR would add: 50
  1180→   - Total: 150 > 100 ✗
  1181→4. Error: "Cannot exceed limit of 100. Already procured 100 as loose item."
  1182→5. User adjusts: Remove Bearing from MR (already covered by loose procurement)
  1183→```
  1184→
  1185→---
  1186→
  1187→## Success Criteria
  1188→
  1189→The architecture is successful if it achieves:
  1190→
  1191→### Functional Goals
  1192→1. ✓ Incremental BOM uploads without manual tracking
  1193→2. ✓ Change detection with impact analysis
  1194→3. ✓ Loose item integration without duplicate procurement
  1195→4. ✓ Procurement quantity control enforcement
  1196→5. ✓ Budget and timeline visibility per component
  1197→6. ✓ Audit trail of BOM versions and changes
  1198→
  1199→### Non-Functional Goals
  1200→1. ✓ Maintainable: Clear separation of concerns (upload logic, validation, reporting)
  1201→2. ✓ Extensible: Can add new procurement controls without core changes
  1202→3. ✓ Performant: Analysis on 500-component BOM < 30 seconds
  1203→4. ✓ User-friendly: Clear error messages, actionable dialogs
  1204→5. ✓ Auditable: Full history of changes, decisions, procurement
  1205→
  1206→### Business Outcomes
  1207→1. Reduced over-procurement (cost savings)
  1208→2. Faster project execution (parallel work on independent components)
  1209→3. Better cost control (budget tracking and variance analysis)
  1210→4. Improved visibility (management dashboards and reports)
  1211→5. Reduced manual coordination (system enforces rules)
  1212→
  1213→---
  1214→
  1215→## Future Enhancements
  1216→
  1217→### Phase 2 Possibilities
  1218→1. **Differential Material Requests**
  1219→   - When BOM changes, auto-generate MR for ONLY new/changed items
  1220→   - Skip items already procured
  1221→
  1222→2. **Procurement Timeline Prediction**
  1223→   - Based on lead times, predict component arrival dates
  1224→   - Flag assemblies blocked by late components
  1225→
  1226→3. **Cost Variance Alerts**
  1227→   - Auto-alert when actual PO cost exceeds budget by >10%
  1228→   - Suggest design alternatives
  1229→
  1230→4. **Supplier Integration**
  1231→   - When BOM changes, auto-notify suppliers of affected RFQs
  1232→   - Request revised quotes
  1233→
  1234→5. **Design Change Workflow**
  1235→   - Formal approval process for BOM changes
  1236→   - Impact analysis report before change approved
  1237→
  1238→---
  1239→
  1240→## Appendix: Key Design Principles
  1241→
  1242→### 1. Explicit Over Implicit
  1243→- Loose items require explicit conversion enabling
  1244→- Changes require explicit user resolution
  1245→- No silent auto-corrections
  1246→
  1247→### 2. Fail Fast and Loud
  1248→- Hard blocks on validation failures
  1249→- Clear error messages with actionable steps
  1250→- Better to stop early than corrupt data
  1251→
  1252→### 3. Single Source of Truth
  1253→- Component Master is THE record for project-specific component data
  1254→- Other doctypes (Item, BOM) remain global/reusable
  1255→- Clear ownership boundaries
  1256→
  1257→### 4. Business Logic in Code, Not User Memory
  1258→- System enforces quantity limits (not user discipline)
  1259→- System detects changes (not user tracking)
  1260→- System calculates rollups (not spreadsheets)
  1261→
  1262→### 5. Optimize for Common Case
  1263→- 90% of uploads are incremental (mostly new, few changed)
  1264→- Allow fast path for common case
  1265→- Slow path only for exceptions
  1266→
  1267→---
  1268→
  1269→## Conclusion
  1270→
  1271→The **Project Component Master** architecture provides a robust, maintainable solution for Clevertech's complex ETO procurement workflow. By introducing a project-specific component tracking layer, the system bridges the gap between design evolution (PE2) and procurement execution (ERPNext).
  1272→
  1273→Key innovations:
  1274→- **Managed components** concept (assemblies + loose items only)
  1275→- **MAX-based quantity limits** (preventing duplicate procurement)
  1276→- **Ancestor chain blocking** (surgical change control)
  1277→- **Hash-based change detection** (fast and reliable)
  1278→
  1279→The design balances flexibility (supports continuous design evolution) with control (enforces procurement limits), enabling Clevertech to scale their ETO operations efficiently.
  1280→
  1281→---
  1282→
  1283→## Implementation Status
  1284→
  1285→**Phase 1: DocTypes Created** ✅ (2026-01-26)
  1286→- Project Component Master (40+ fields, 2 child tables)
  1287→- Component Procurement Record (child table)
  1288→- Component BOM Usage (child table)
  1289→- All fields migrated and visible in ERPNext
  1290→
  1291→**Phase 1A: BOM Event Hooks** ✅ (2026-01-26)
  1292→- on_submit, on_cancel, on_update_after_submit hooks
  1293→- Auto-populates BOM Usage child table
  1294→- Hash-based structure change detection
  1295→- Info messages for untracked project BOMs
  1296→- Silent skip pattern for non-project BOMs
  1297→- **File:** `project_component_master/bom_hooks.py`
  1298→
  1299→**Phase 2A: Bulk Generation (Cutover Flow)** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1300→- "Generate Component Masters" button on Project form
  1301→- Creates Component Masters FROM existing BOMs (BOM → CM direction)
  1302→- Tested on project SMR260001: 24 Component Masters created
  1303→- **Files:** `project_component_master/bulk_generation.py`, `public/js/project.js`
  1304→
  1305→**Phase 2: Enhanced BOM Upload (New Project Flow)** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1306→- New "Create BOMs with Validation" button on BOM Upload form
  1307→- Full analysis engine: parse Excel → create Items → create Component Masters → analyze → create BOMs → link back
  1308→- Hash-based change detection categorizes components as new/unchanged/changed/blocked
  1309→- Ancestor chain blocking — changed child blocks all parent assemblies
  1310→- Loose item blocking — items without `can_be_converted_to_bom` block upload
  1311→- Three dialog outcomes: blocked (red), requires resolution (orange), success (green)
  1312→- Imports existing `parse_rows`, `build_tree`, `ensure_item_exists`, `create_bom_recursive` from `bom_upload.py` (Decision 12 — no modification to existing code)
  1313→- **Files:**
  1314→  - `clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload_enhanced.py` — Server logic (whitelisted)
  1315→  - `clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload.js` — Button handler + 3 dialog functions
  1316→  - `clevertech/doctype/bom_upload/bom_upload.json` — Added button field
  1317→
  1318→**Phase 3: Procurement Hooks** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1319→- 8 event hooks: on_submit and on_cancel for Material Request, RFQ, Purchase Order, Purchase Receipt
  1320→- Automatically adds/removes rows in Component Procurement Record child table
  1321→- Tracks: document_type, document_name, quantity, rate, amount, date, status, procurement_source
  1322→- Silent skip pattern — only processes items with an existing Component Master
  1323→- Idempotent — duplicate records checked before inserting
  1324→- Supplier Quotations NOT tracked (use separate comparison report per design)
  1325→- **File:** `project_component_master/procurement_hooks.py`
  1326→
  1327→**Phase 3A: Material Request Quantity Validation** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1328→- Validate hook on Material Request — checks qty against `total_qty_limit`
  1329→- Hard block (frappe.throw) if cumulative MR qty would exceed limit
  1330→- Calculates existing MR qty across all non-cancelled MRs for the project+item
  1331→- Warning for loose item over-procurement (loose qty > BOM qty)
  1332→- Clear error table: total limit, existing MRs, this MR, max allowed
  1333→- **File:** `project_component_master/material_request_validation.py`
  1334→
  1335→**Phase 4: Auto-Calculations** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1336→- `before_save()` in ProjectComponentMaster class runs 6 auto-calculations:
  1337→  - `calculate_bom_qty_required()` — sums qty_per_unit from BOM Usage table (**Bug: does not multiply by parent's project_qty — see Phase 4B**)
  1338→  - `calculate_total_qty_limit()` — MAX(loose_qty, bom_qty)
  1339→  - `calculate_procurement_totals()` — sums MR + PO quantities from procurement records
  1340→  - `update_procurement_status()` — Not Started / In Progress / Completed / Over-procured
  1341→  - `calculate_budgeted_rate_rollup()` — child component budgets + leaf item last purchase rates
  1342→  - `update_bom_conversion_status()` — Not Applicable / Pending / Converted / Partial
  1343→- **File:** `clevertech/doctype/project_component_master/project_component_master.py`
  1344→
  1345→**Phase 4B: Project Quantity Multiplication** ⏳ (Pending — Decision 13)
  1346→- **Gap identified:** `bom_qty_required` sums raw `qty_per_unit` without multiplying by parent assembly's project quantity
  1347→- Add `project_qty` field to Project Component Master (editable Float, not read-only)
  1348→- Fix `calculate_bom_qty_required()` to compute `total_qty_required = qty_per_unit × parent_project_qty`
  1349→- BOM Upload flow: auto-set `project_qty` by walking tree top-down after upload
  1350→- Cutover flow: defaults to 1, user must manually enter project_qty on root assemblies
  1351→- **Cutover limitation:** Root assemblies have no bom_usage rows and no Excel source. Raw materials have no CMs from cutover. Full multiplication only works after user sets root project_qty and raw material CMs exist.
  1352→- **Files:** `project_component_master.json`, `project_component_master.py`, `bom_upload_enhanced.py`, `bulk_generation.py`
  1353→
  1354→**Phase 4A: Hooks Registration** ✅ (2026-01-27)
  1355→- All new hooks registered in hooks.py using list syntax where events have existing handlers
  1356→- Purchase Receipt on_submit: existing QI handler + new procurement hook (list)
  1357→- Material Request validate: existing stock validation + new qty limit check (list)
  1358→- New on_submit/on_cancel events added for PO, RFQ
  1359→- **File:** `hooks.py`
  1360→
  1361→**Phase 5: Reports** ⏳ (Pending)
  1362→- Component Procurement Status report
  1363→- Report Python logic + JavaScript filters/formatters
  1364→
  1365→**Phase 6: Testing** ⏳ (Pending)
  1366→- Unit tests for Component Master auto-calculations
  1367→- Integration tests for enhanced BOM Upload
  1368→- MR validation tests
  1369→
  1370→---
  1371→
  1372→**Document Version:** 1.6
  1373→**Last Updated:** 2026-01-27
  1374→**Authors:** Saket-TT & BharatBodh Team
  1375→**Status:** Phases 1–4A Complete. Phase 4B (project_qty multiplication) pending — critical fix for bom_qty_required. Reports and testing pending.
  1376→
